title: Orloj
starterQuestions: How does the clock mechanism work?
How do deviation tokens affect my score?
How does the solo mode work?

chunks.content: Each player constructs the signs of the zodiac and the calendar months, as well as the images of the apostles. With each construction, you will be one step closer to completing this masterpiece.

General components: 1 Main board, 1 Orloj clock face, 1 Astronomical dial, 1 Moon disc, 1 Walk of the apostles, 2 Apostle gears, 1 Painter, 1 Golden rooster, 1 Round marker, 2 Hammer discs, 1 Large final construction dial, 3 Orloj crowning pieces, 3 Plastic rotation axes (1 spare), 12 Zodiac dials, 6 Blocked zodiac dials, 12 Month dials, 6 Blocked month dials, 12 Calendar sections, 6 Mastery stained-glass windows, 9 Common stained-glass window objectives (3 of each colour), 9 Common objective bonuses (3 of each colour), 6 Royal scrolls (blue seal), 24 Scrolls (red seal), 21 Workshop cards, 6 Hammer cards, 8 Initial resource cards, 4 Additional actions reference cards, 18 Assistants (3 of each type), 15 Gold pieces, 20 Paint pieces, 20 Wood pieces, 20 Iron pieces, 18 Coins, 1 Bag.
Solo mode additional components: 18 Action cards, 3 Basic turn scoring cards, 3 Advanced turn scoring cards, 1 Endgame scoring card, 5 Scoring tiles, 3 Mastery tiles, 3 Mastery track tiles, 3 End-of-round action tiles, 1 Conditions met tile, 1 Mastery track objective tile.
Player components (per player): 1 Personal board, 1 Forge, 1 Warehouse, 3 Production cubes, 1 Hammer cube, 1 Rooster disc, 14 Workers, 1 Sculptor, 3 Mastery discs, 1 Scoring marker, 1 Personal set of 12 apostle tiles (numbered 1–12, backs in player's colour), 5 Deviation tokens.

Walk of the apostles: Assemble the gears onto the walk of the apostles. Fit the gears together so that in the lower position, Apostle 1 (James) is displayed on the left side and Apostle 2 (Peter) on the right side. This is the initial position for each game. Use the plastic axes for assembly.
Common objective bonuses: Take the common objective bonuses and separate them by the colour of their backs. Shuffle each colour separately and place one of each colour in the corresponding positions according to their backs. Return the rest to the box.

1. Unfold the main board in the centre. Place the Orloj clock face and the astronomical dial on the rotation axis to form the clock.
2. Shuffle the 21 workshop cards; place 3 in the designated spaces on the board. Set the rest aside.
3. Take the month dials, place them in the bag. Draw at random the number indicated by the player count. Place a blocked month dial in each construction area space indicated by the drawn dials. Draw 1 more dial and place all extracted dials in the calendar (illustrated side up). Place remaining dials in the construction area (non-illustrated side up). Prepare the zodiac dials the same way.
4. Place the moon disc in the first position to the left of the hand inside the astronomical dial. Set the Orloj clock face in a random position and the astronomical dial with the hand pointing to the starting hour.
5. Place the walk of the apostles above the board. Rotate the gears until apostles 1 and 2 are visible at the bottom. Randomly take one Orloj crowning piece and assemble it with the walk of the apostles (note: one crowning piece is only for solo mode). Place the round marker on the leftmost space of the round track according to the number of players. Place the golden rooster on the general rooster track on the number indicated by the round marker.
6. Shuffle the 6 mastery stained-glass windows; randomly place 3 at the top of the mastery tracks. Return the rest.
7. Place the large final construction dial in the centre of the calendar (correct side up). Place a hammer disc in the indicated position in each construction zone (months and zodiac).
8. Shuffle the 6 royal scrolls; place 3 face up at the top of the mastery tracks. Return the rest.
9. Shuffle the 12 calendar sections; place them face up in a random circle around the calendar. For the first game: store the 12 sections and use the setup printed on the board.
10. Take scrolls (red seal) by player count: use all in a four-player game; remove scrolls marked with the 3-player symbol in a three-player game; also remove those marked with the 2-player symbol in a two-player or solo game. Shuffle into a face-down reserve; reveal 3 (1–2 players) or 4 (3+ players).
11. Separate common stained-glass-window objectives by back colour; shuffle each colour and place one of each colour in corresponding positions. Return the rest.
12. Place resources (gold, paint, wood, iron, coins) next to the board as a general supply.
13. Place the painter in the indicated position. Place assistants in 6 piles (same iconography each); each pile has 1 assistant fewer than the number of players (in solo or 2-player, 1 assistant of each type). Return any remaining to the box.

Each player performs these steps:
1. Place your personal board in the centre of your play area, along with your warehouse and forge.
2. Take your personal set of apostle tiles (back in your colour, numbered 1–12) and place them next to your personal board.
3. Deal each player an additional actions reference card.
4. Place your 5 deviation tokens at the top of your personal board with the side that does NOT show the broken gear icon facing up.
5. Place your rooster disc in the first position of your personal rooster track, starting from the bottom.
6. Place one cube in the bottom position of each production track and your forge track.
7. Place workers in the indicated positions: 3 in the apostle panel, 3 in the production zone, 1 above each workshop card on the main board. Place the rest of your workers next to your personal board as available workers. In three- and four-player games, return one of these workers to the box (each player has four available workers in those games).
8. Place your sculptor next to your personal board.
9. Place one mastery disc on the lowest space of each mastery track on the main board.
10. Place your scoring marker on space 10 of the scoring track.
11. Take one gold from the general supply and place it in your personal reserve.
The last player to have visited the city of Prague is the starting player.

chunks.section: Game Setup — Hammers and Initial Resources
chunks.content:
Shuffle the hammer cards and reveal one more than the number of players. Place one initial resource card next to each hammer card to form combinations. Starting with the last player and proceeding counterclockwise, each player chooses one combination.
Each player places their hammer card underneath their forge and takes the resources indicated on the initial resource card. If the initial resource card also includes apostles, take them from your personal set and place them in your warehouse.
You may wait to take the resources from your initial resource card until just before your first turn to know the starting positions of the astronomical dial and the Orloj clock face.
For the first game: instead of assembling combinations randomly, use the four preset initial setups, following the number in the lower right corner of each card. Randomly distribute one combination to each player.

There are two types of scoring: immediate scoring and endgame scoring (referred to as VP).
- Immediate VP icon: Scoring is resolved immediately. Move your marker forward on the scoring track as many spaces as indicated.
- Endgame VP icon: Scoring is resolved at the end of the game. Do not move your marker yet.
Flip your scoring marker to indicate a score greater than 100 VP.

A resource icon indicates you take a resource from the general supply into your play area. A resource icon crossed out indicates a cost you must pay by returning the resource to the general supply.
Basic resources: Paint, wood and iron. A wildcard basic resource icon means one basic resource of your choice: paint, wood, or iron.
Special resources: Gold (essential for construction) and coins (related to mastery tracks). You can pay a special resource instead of any basic resource. You cannot pay coins instead of gold, or gold instead of coins.
Resource exchange: You can exchange three identical basic resources for one gold at any time during your turn.

Each player has 5 deviation tokens on their personal board. They start with the non-broken-gear side face up.
Forcing deviation: Flip one deviation token to reveal the broken gear icon. You cannot force a deviation if all 5 tokens already show the broken gear. Whenever a force deviation icon appears next to an action, you must first force the deviation before performing the rest of the action.
Correcting deviation: Flip a deviation token back to hide the broken gear icon.
Forcing deviation allows you to perform additional movements beyond permitted limits (e.g., extra rotations of the astronomical dial or clock face, extra gear rotations). Broken gear tokens cause VP loss during the rooster's call and at endgame.

Workers are a key resource. Without available workers you must pass or find ways to recover or unlock workers.
Recovering workers: Take workers that were used on the clock or calendar and make them available again. Note: workers are normally recovered only from the clock. Recovering from the calendar may result in fewer VP at end of game.
- Recover 1 worker icon: Recover one worker.
- Recover 2 workers icon: Recover two workers.
- Recover N workers icon: Recover the indicated number.
Unlocking new workers (methods):
- Apostle panel: unlock a worker when you complete certain placements (section 10.1).
- Workshop cards: unlock the worker above a purchased workshop card (section 8.7).
- Production zone upgrades: unlock a worker once all three production cubes reach that worker's position (section 8.6).

There are three mastery tracks: Innovation (blue), Precision (pink), and Observation (yellow). Each track improves based on the position of your mastery disc.
- Innovation Track (blue): Indicates the maximum number of spaces the astronomical dial may rotate without forcing deviation.
- Precision Track (pink): Determines the number of workers recovered when you pass, and your sculptor's level.
- Observation Track (yellow): Determines the maximum number of steps the painter can move and the steps when activating the moon.
Bonuses on mastery tracks: When your mastery disc reaches a bonus position on a track, you immediately receive it:
- Moon bonus: Gain one moon activation action.
- Assistant bonus: Take an assistant from one of the stacks and place it in the designated space in your warehouse. You cannot have two assistants with the same iconography.
- Scroll (red seal) bonus: Take one scroll from those revealed on the main board, then replace it with a new one from the pile.
- Royal Scroll (blue seal) bonus: Score immediately based on the condition on the scroll, up to 10 VP. No other player can score a royal scroll that has already been scored.
- Sculptor Level indicator: Shows your sculptor's level for actions with that piece.

The clock has three moving parts:
- Outer Ring: Printed on the main board; divided into sections. Each section provides a specific action.
- Orloj clock face: Divided into sections; always moves counterclockwise. Each section has a resource-related effect.
- Astronomical dial: Always moves clockwise. Its hand points to which outer ring section and which clock face section are active.
- Moon disc: Moves clockwise along the astronomical dial. Passing it over moon reward spaces gains those rewards and is the primary way to correct deviations.
Note: The astronomical dial and the Orloj clock face are two independent components. Rotating one does not affect the other.

The warehouse holds apostles and assistants from your personal board. It has two spaces: one exclusively for an apostle, and one that can hold either an apostle or an assistant. You may rearrange apostles and assistants in your warehouse at any time.
You cannot take an apostle (action 8.3) if your warehouse is full.

The game begins with the starting player's turn. Players take turns in clockwise order until the game ends.
On each turn, you have two options:
1. Activate the clock mechanism (requires at least one available worker)
2. Pass
Regardless of the option chosen, at any point during your turn you may perform any number of additional actions (see section 10).

You must have at least one available worker. Configure the clock so the hand of the astronomical dial points to a section of the outer ring and a section of the Orloj clock face.
Steps:
1. Configure the astronomical dial: It always rotates clockwise and must move at least one space. The maximum spaces it can rotate without forcing deviation equals your disc height on the blue mastery track. Force as many deviations as the extra spaces you wish to move beyond that limit.
2. Configure the Orloj clock face: It always rotates counterclockwise. You are not required to move it. Force as many deviations as the number of spaces you wish to rotate it.
3. Place one of your available workers on the space indicated by the hand. If another worker is already there, return it to its owner (you may return your own worker to yourself).
4. Choose whether to perform the outer ring section action first or the Orloj clock face section action first. You cannot alternate between sections.
Note: The two dials are independent; rotating one does not affect the other.

chunks.section: Outer Ring Sections and Clock Face Sections
chunks.content:
Outer Ring Section actions (triggered when the hand lands on that section):
- Produce resources: Produce resources as indicated in your corresponding production track.
- Recover workers: Recover workers from the clock or calendar (see section 5.4.1).
- Take coin + apostle: Take one coin and one apostle (see section 8.3).
- Double mastery + coin: Pay one coin to move your disc up one space on a mastery track of your choice AND move your disc up one space on the indicated mastery track. Perform these in any order.
Clock Face Section effects (triggered when the Orloj clock face section aligns):
- Spend basic resource: Spend one paint, one wood, or one iron of your choice (cost).
- Spend indicated resource: Spend the specific resource shown (cost).
- Take indicated resource: Gain the specific resource shown.
- Produce from any production track: Produce the resources indicated in a production track of your choice.
- Produce from corresponding production track: Produce the resources indicated in your corresponding production track.

Instead of activating the clock mechanism, you may pass. You may pass even if you still have available workers.
The pass action is tied to the height of your disc on the pink mastery track and has three mandatory steps:
1. Recover as many workers as indicated by your disc height on the pink mastery track.
2. Activate the moon (see section 8.4).
3. Move the golden rooster one space to the right on the general rooster track.

Produce as many resources as indicated by your production track.
- Wildcard resource icon: Take one resource of your choice (paint, wood, or iron).
- Production track icon: Produce resources from a production track of your choice.
- Corresponding production track icon: Produce resources from a specific production track as indicated.

Option A: Move one of your discs up one space on the mastery track indicated by the icon colour.
Option B: Pay one coin and move one of your discs up one space on a mastery track of your choice.
Bonus: Gain one VP if, upon receiving the mastery, your disc was already on the last space of that track.

Take one of the two apostles displayed at the bottom of the gears from your personal set and place it in your warehouse. Then rotate the gears one position to reveal a new combination. The rotation direction is indicated at the centre of the gears.
You cannot take an apostle if your warehouse is full.
Before taking an apostle, you may optionally rotate the gears extra times by forcing deviation — force one deviation token per extra space you wish to rotate.
After taking the apostle, the gears automatically rotate one additional position.
Rooster movement: Every time one of the red teeth of the left gear aligns with the red arrow during any gear rotation, move the rooster one position to the right on the general rooster track. If the rooster reaches the last position, proceed to the rooster's call (section 9).

Move the moon disc clockwise along the astronomical dial as many steps as indicated by your disc height on the yellow mastery track. Gain all rewards shown in the spaces the moon passes through (including the landing space).
Modifier icon: When a moon icon shows a modifier (e.g., +2), activate the moon and gain that many additional steps beyond what your yellow mastery disc indicates.
Note: The moon is the primary way to correct deviations, as many moon spaces show the correct-deviation reward.

Move the painter clockwise around the calendar sections by up to the number of steps indicated by your disc height on the yellow mastery track. The painter must move at least one space. After moving, choose one of the two bonuses shown on the calendar sections to the left or right of the painter's new position.

Move one of your production cubes or your hammer cube one position upward on its track.
Unlocking workers: In your production zone, a worker becomes available once all three production cubes reach that worker's position. Take the worker and add it to your available workers.
Bonus: Gain 1 VP and 1 gold when performing an upgrade on a production track that has already reached its final position.

Scroll (red seal) action: Take one scroll from the face-up scrolls on the main board, then replace it with a new one from the face-down pile. Scrolls can be used as additional actions during your turn. Keep the scroll in your play area whether or not you have used it.
Royal Scroll (blue seal) action: Score immediately based on the condition on the scroll, up to a maximum of 10 VP. Keep it in your play area. No other player can score a royal scroll that has already been scored.

Steps to expand your workshop:
1. Choose a workshop card and pay the resources shown at the bottom. The cost always includes one paint plus additional resources based on the card's position and type.
2. If there is a worker of your colour above the card, unlock it and add it to your available workers. If no worker is present, you have already bought a card at that position and do not receive a new worker.
3. Gain the reward shown at the top of the card.
4. Take the card into your play area. If it is your first workshop card, simply place it; otherwise place it to the left or right of your existing workshop cards.
5. If placing the new card completes the two halves of an apostle icon or a construction icon on your workshop cards, immediately perform that action.
6. Slide all workshop cards to the right and refill the empty space with a new card from the deck.

There are two construction zones: month dials and zodiac dials. The process is identical for both.
1. Move the hammer disc clockwise (following the blue arrows) to a position adjacent to the dial you wish to construct. Maximum hammer movement equals your cube's position on your hammer card. Force one deviation per additional space beyond that limit. You are not required to move the hammer disc.
2. Pay the resources shown at the intersection of the dial's row and column. Gain 2 VP for each resource spent.
3. Place the constructed tile (illustrated side up) in its calendar position. Place one available worker on it and gain 2 VP per dial (including this one) in an adjacent group of dials that each have one of your workers on them. A single dial with no adjacent workers counts as a group of 1.
   - Note: Month dials and zodiac dials are adjacent to each other (e.g., month XII is adjacent to month I, month XI, and the Capricorn zodiac dial).
   - Note: You must have an available worker to construct.
4. Gain the bonus of the calendar section where the dial was built.
5. Gain the hammer bonus based on your cube's position on your hammer card (see Appendix: Hammers).

Move your sculptor to one of the four sculptor action spaces on the main board. If another sculptor is already there, return it to its owner, who places it back in their play area. You must move the sculptor — you cannot repeat the same space if your sculptor is already there.
Your sculptor's level is determined by your disc's height on the pink mastery track. Perform the action corresponding to your sculptor's level.
Some sculptor actions require forcing a deviation first — you cannot perform the rest of the action unless you have forced it.
The specific effects at each sculptor space and level are listed in the Appendix: Sculptor Actions.

The rooster moves right on the general rooster track for only two reasons:
1. When rotating the apostle gears, a red tooth on the left gear aligns with the red arrow — move the rooster one step.
2. When a player passes their turn — move the rooster one step.
When the rooster reaches the last position: The player whose turn it is takes the rooster as a reminder and finishes their turn as usual. Any further rooster steps during the same turn are ignored. After the turn ends, resolve in order:
1. All players correct as many deviations as indicated by their personal rooster track. If the track value is higher than the player's current broken gear count, gain 1 VP for each deviation that cannot be corrected.
2. All players lose VP equal to the number of broken gear icons still showing on their deviation tokens.
3. Move the round marker one position to the right on the round track. Place the rooster back on the general rooster track at the number indicated by the new round marker position. The game continues.

Additional actions may be performed at any time during your turn, even while passing. There is no limit to how many you may perform. Additional actions include:
- Placing an apostle (10.1)
- Spending a coin (10.2)
- Using a scroll (10.3)
- Placing an assistant (10.4)
- Claiming an objective (10.5)
- Exchanging resources for gold (10.6)
- Taking one coin and performing an upgrade
- Recovering workers from the clock or calendar
- Activating the moon or moving the painter
- Expanding the workshop, performing a construction, or activating the sculptor
- Placing a worker on a common stained-glass-window objective

To place an apostle it must come from your warehouse. Pay the cost shown to the left of the row on your apostle panel where you wish to place it. The apostle tile must match the colour of the space. Once placed, immediately receive the bonus shown below that space.
Bonuses for completing a row or column in the apostle panel (immediately upon completion):
- Complete a row: Gain 7 VP and perform a production on one of your production tracks of your choice.
- Complete a column: Activate the moon OR take an assistant.
Types of placement bonuses on individual spaces (examples):
- Perform an upgrade.
- Recover two workers.
- Unlock the worker in that space and add it to your available workers.

Spend one coin to use it as any resource except gold.
Additionally, there is a separate action to take one coin and perform an upgrade (take 1 coin from the supply and simultaneously move one production cube or hammer cube one position upward).
Flip over a scroll tile from your play area to gain the bonus printed on it. Keep the used scroll in your play area face down (used). All scroll effects are listed in the Appendix: Scrolls.

Pay the resource indicated below a workshop card to place an assistant from your warehouse onto that card. Gain the bonus shown in the centre of the card. Each workshop card has only one assistant space. Assistants provide additional endgame scoring. Rewards gained by placing an assistant are listed in the Appendix: Rewards for Placing an Assistant.

Each game has three common stained-glass-window objectives, each showing a condition. When you fulfill the condition, you may (as an additional action) place one of your available workers in the uppermost available position below that objective.
- If you are the first to claim it, receive the bonus on the bonus tile below, then flip the tile over. No other player receives this bonus for the same objective.
- Each player may claim each objective only once.
- Objectives have three reward positions: 1st = 8 VP, 2nd = 6 VP, 3rd = 4 VP (scored at endgame).
- In a two-player game or solo mode, the second player to claim must place their worker in the third position (the second position is unavailable).

chunks.section: Additional Action — Exchanging Resources for Gold
chunks.content: Exchange three identical basic resources for one gold. This may be performed at any time during your turn.

The game ends after completing the round in which either:
- The last calendar dial is constructed, OR
- The fourth rooster's call occurs.
In both cases, complete the round so all players have taken the same number of turns, then proceed to endgame scoring.
The player with the highest score wins. Tie-breaker: the player who has placed the most apostles on their apostle panel. If still tied, those players share the victory.

When a player performs the last construction of a dial on the calendar, they flip the large final construction dial to enable a special construction. On any subsequent turn, any player may use a construct action to pay 1 gold + 1 wood + 1 paint + 1 iron to gain 8 VP. No worker is placed on this tile. No adjacency points or calendar section bonuses are awarded, but you still receive your hammer bonus.

A scoring summary is printed on the game board. Endgame scoring has five categories:
1. Mastery Stained-Glass-Window Scoring: Each mastery window scores independently. Your mastery disc must have reached the seventh space (brick line) on the corresponding track to score. Each window has a Scoring Condition, Primary Scoring (highest disc on that track, or tied players), and Secondary Scoring (other players who have crossed the brick line). Score the condition as many times as you meet it; VP per condition depends on your disc height.
2. Surplus Gold Scoring: Score 1 VP for each gold in your play area.
3. Deviation Scoring: Lose 1 VP for each broken gear icon still showing on your deviation tokens. Note: Your personal rooster track does not modify this.
4. Assistant Scoring: Score each assistant placed on a workshop card (see Appendix: Assistants).
5. Common Stained-Glass-Window Objective Scoring: 8 VP for the first position, 6 VP for the second, 4 VP for the third on each objective.

In solo mode you play against Josef Mánes. Josef Mánes always plays second each round. Play your turn as usual, then perform Josef Mánes' turn. After Josef Mánes' turns 3, 6, and 9, perform a maintenance phase. After turn 12, proceed to endgame scoring.
Josef Mánes does not gain or spend resources. He does not receive rewards for expanding the workshop, constructing, claiming objectives, or advancing on mastery tracks — except he takes scrolls and scores royal scrolls when reaching those positions. He does not perform the action indicated by the astronomical dial hand. He only follows his action cards. He does not force or correct deviations. There are no resources in his play area.

Make these changes to the standard setup:
- Replace step 3 of general setup: Use the solo mode Orloj crowning piece. Place the round marker on space 4. Place the rooster on space 4 of the general rooster track. After each rooster's call the rooster resets to position 4. The round marker does not move.
- Remove the mastery stained-glass windows, royal scrolls, and common stained-glass-window objectives indicated for solo mode before placing them.
- Replace step 4 of individual player setup: Place one cube at the first position from the bottom of the forge track. Place one cube in the second position of each production track. This unlocks a sixth worker for solo mode.
- Do not use a personal board for Josef Mánes. He uses only his 14 workers (placed as available workers), one mastery disc per track, and his scoring marker on space 10.
- Place Josef Mánes' endgame scoring card to the right of his play area.
- Take the 12 action cards from after general setup and place them face up.
- Shuffle the 3 basic turn scoring cards face-down in position.
- Shuffle the 3 mastery tiles of Josef Mánes; reveal one per position.
- Shuffle the 3 mastery track tiles; reveal one per position.
- Shuffle the 5 scoring tiles; reveal 3, return the rest.
- Shuffle the 3 end-of-round action tiles; reveal one per position.
- Place the conditions met tile and mastery track objective tile in Josef Mánes' play area.

chunks.section: Solo Mode — Josef Mánes Component Setup (Step 11)
chunks.content:
Replace steps 10 and 11 of general setup with the following (this step is also repeated after each of Josef Mánes' turns and at the start of each player turn):
1. Take the solo mode action card deck, shuffle it and reveal 6 cards.
2. Place the zodiac and month dials shown on these cards directly onto the calendar (face up). Place blocked zodiac and month dials in the construction area on spaces indicated by the revealed cards. Place remaining dials in construction area (non-illustrated side up).
3. In solo mode, there is only one set of apostles shared between both players. Separate the apostle tiles that appear on the revealed cards — these are unavailable during the solo game.
4. Return the 6 revealed action cards to the box. They are not used this game. The remaining 12 cards are used for play.
5. Place the top action card in the first available position and reveal a scoring card in the first available position. Repeat after each of Josef Mánes' turns in the next available space. If no space is available, first resolve the end-of-round phase (rounds 3, 6, or 9) before performing this step again.

chunks.section: Solo Mode — Josef Mánes' Turn Structure
chunks.content:
All action cards have the following structure:
- Required workers to execute the card
- Astronomical dial and Orloj clock face rotations
- First action of Josef Mánes + its reward
- Second action of Josef Mánes + its reward
Josef Mánes carries out his action card as follows:
1. Check that he has the required available workers. If not, he recovers all workers from the clock and ends his turn (perform step 11, then proceed with your turn).
2. Perform the rotations and place one of his workers on the outer ring section the hand points to. If one of your workers is already there, return it to your play area.
3. Perform the first action. If successful, gain the first action's reward.
4. Perform the second action. If successful, gain the second action's reward.
5. Perform the action check (section 13.8).
Action types on his cards:
- Move the painter: Move the painter the indicated number of spaces clockwise.
- Construct a zodiac or month dial: Place the dial directly on the calendar with its unconstructed side up (no illustration).
- Advance on a mastery track: Move two steps up on the indicated track.
- Take an apostle: Take the indicated apostle from the shared set; rotate the gears one position.
- Take a workshop card: Take the rightmost or leftmost workshop card as indicated; refill the space.
- Take an assistant: Take the leftmost or rightmost available assistant as indicated.

chunks.section: Solo Mode — Action Check and Rewards
chunks.content:
If Josef Mánes successfully performs all actions on his card, proceed with the action check:
1. Flip over the dial(s) he constructed; place one of his workers on each and gain 2 VP per dial in an adjacent group with his workers (same adjacency rules as normal construction). If two constructions: resolve top one first, score, then bottom one.
2. Score the points from the basic turn scoring card; move his score marker accordingly and move the rooster as indicated. If the rooster reaches the last position, perform the rooster's call as usual after his turn. Ignore any extra rooster steps he cannot take. Josef Mánes does not lose points from the rooster's call.
3. After the action check, perform step 11 of Josef Mánes' component setup and proceed with your next turn.
If Josef Mánes does NOT successfully perform all actions: flip over his constructed dials and end his turn.
Josef Mánes' rewards when reaching track bonuses:
- If he reaches a scroll space: take a scroll from the face-down pile.
- If he reaches a royal scroll space: score it immediately.
- He does NOT take assistants or activate the moon from mastery track bonuses.
- If his mastery disc is already on the last position of a track: gain 1 VP per step he would have advanced.

chunks.section: Solo Mode — End of Rounds 3, 6 and 9
chunks.content:
After completing Josef Mánes' turn in round 3, 6, or 9, follow these steps in order:
1. Following the end-of-round action tile, place one of his workers in the first available position of the indicated stained-glass-window objective regardless of whether he met the condition. If one of your workers is already in that position, place his in the third position. If he has no available workers, first retrieve all his workers from the Orloj clock face. Note: The icon number on the tile indicates which objective: (1) is the leftmost.
2. Move the rooster one position; perform the rooster's call if necessary.
3. Check Josef Mánes' mastery disc on the track indicated by the mastery track tile. If his disc is lower than the value on the mastery track objective tile, flip the scoring tile to its 0 VP side. If the same or higher, do not flip it. Note: The first space from the bottom is space 0.
4. Receive the mastery bonuses from the mastery tiles (2 masteries for basic level; 3 for medium and advanced levels).
5. Flip the mastery track tile and end-of-round action tile to show the hourglass icon.
6. Discard the three action cards and reshuffle the turn scoring cards. Remove the three visible scrolls from the board and reveal new ones.
7. Perform step 11 of Josef Mánes' component setup. Move on to your next turn.

chunks.section: Solo Mode — End of Solo Game and Scoring
chunks.content:
The solo game ends immediately after Josef Mánes' 12th turn. Perform your own scoring as usual.
To win, you must score more points than Josef Mánes. In a tie, Josef Mánes wins.
To score Josef Mánes, follow his endgame scoring card:
1. Exchange all his resources and coins to maximize gold (three identical resources = one gold; coins count as any resource type). Score 1 VP per gold.
2. Score mastery stained-glass windows as usual.
3. Score 3 VP per assistant in his play area (6 VP per assistant in advanced mode).
4. Score the scoring tiles that were NOT flipped to 0 VP.
5. Score common stained-glass-window objectives as usual.
Josef Mánes can score up to 15 VP per scoring tile. All his scoring tiles are listed in the Appendix: Josef Mánes' Scoring Tiles.

Many solo mode components contain the advanced symbol. You can customize difficulty by adding or flipping components with this symbol.
- Basic Level: Play without any components marked with the advanced symbol.
- Medium Level Option 1: Flip the mastery tiles and the mastery track objective tile to show the advanced symbol.
- Medium Level Option 2: Use the 3 advanced turn scoring cards instead of the basic ones and flip the endgame scoring card to its advanced side.
- Advanced Level: Combine Medium Level Option 1 and Medium Level Option 2.

- Avoid spreading across all three mastery tracks. Focus on two. The best combination depends on which common stained-glass-window objectives and mastery stained-glass windows are in play.
- Do not exhaust all your deviation tokens. Use the moon to correct deviations when needed. Passing is not always bad — it activates the moon.
- Before constructing, identify which calendar section bonus you want and verify you have the necessary resources.
- Pay attention to common stained-glass-window objectives. Being the first to claim them grants bonus rewards and additional scoring.

Each of the four sculptor spaces has a different action. Your sculptor's level is determined by your disc height on the pink mastery track.
Sculptor Space 1 — Gold and Coins:
- Level 1: Force one deviation → take one gold and one coin.
- Level 2: Force one deviation → take one gold and two coins.
- Level 3: Correct one deviation → take one gold and three coins.
Sculptor Space 2 — Painter / Moon:
- Level 1: Force one deviation and move the painter; OR activate the moon without forcing a deviation.
- Level 2: Force one deviation, take one coin, and move the painter; OR take one coin and activate the moon with a +1 modifier.
- Level 3: Correct one deviation, take one coin, and move the painter; OR take one coin and activate the moon with a +2 modifier.
Sculptor Space 3 — Workshop:
- Level 1: Force one deviation → expand your workshop.
- Level 2: Force one deviation, take one resource of your choice → expand your workshop.
- Level 3: Correct one deviation, take one gold → expand your workshop.
Sculptor Space 4 — Construct:
- Level 1: Force two deviations → construct.
- Level 2: Force one deviation, take one resource of your choice → construct.
- Level 3: Correct one deviation, take one gold → construct.
Note: You must force the required deviation(s) before performing the rest of the action. If you cannot force deviation, you cannot perform the action.

Each assistant provides a different endgame scoring bonus. Assistants must be placed on workshop cards to score. There are 6 assistant types:
- Assistant A: Grants 6 VP at the end of the game (flat bonus).
- Assistant B: Grants 1 VP for each upgrade in your production tracks or hammer track at the end of the game.
- Assistant C: Grants 4 VP for each mastery disc at Level IV on any mastery track at the end of the game.
- Assistant D: Grants 5 VP for each completed apostle column in your apostle panel at the end of the game.
- Assistant E: Grants 3 VP for each month dial with one of your workers on top at the end of the game.
- Assistant F: Grants 3 VP for each zodiac dial with one of your workers on top at the end of the game.

When you are the first player to claim a common stained-glass-window objective, you receive the bonus shown on the bonus tile. Possible bonuses:
- Take one apostle.
- Take two coins from the general supply.
- Take two resources of your choice from the general supply.
- Perform an upgrade on your hammer track or one production track of your choice.
- Move two steps up in one mastery track, OR move one step up in each of two different mastery tracks of your choice.

The seven possible common stained-glass-window objective conditions:
1. Have three apostles of the indicated colour placed on your apostle panel.
2. Have two assistants placed on your workshop cards.
3. Have three workshop cards in your play area.
4. Have five upgrades across your hammer track and production tracks.
5. Have one of your mastery discs at Level III.
6. Have two month dials and one zodiac dial constructed, each with one of your workers on top.
7. Have two zodiac dials and one month dial constructed, each with one of your workers on top.

Each mastery stained-glass window scores at end of game (only if your disc has reached or passed the seventh space/brick line on the corresponding track). Points per condition met depend on your disc height. Maximum 15 VP per window.
The six window conditions:
1. Score per scroll or royal scroll (used or unused) in your play area.
2. Score per apostle placed on your apostle panel.
3. Score per upgrade in your hammer track and production tracks.
4. Score per month dial with one of your workers on top.
5. Score per zodiac dial with one of your workers on top.
6. Score per workshop card in your play area.
Primary scoring: The player whose disc is highest on the corresponding track multiplies the primary VP value by times they meet the condition. Tied players all use the primary value.
Secondary scoring: All other players who have crossed the brick line multiply the secondary VP value by times they meet the condition.

Royal scrolls (blue seal) score immediately when claimed and are kept in your play area. No other player can score a royal scroll already scored. Maximum 10 VP each.
Six royal scroll types:
1. Score 3 VP per scroll or royal scroll (including this one) in your play area (used or unused), up to 10 VP.
2. Score 2 VP per apostle placed on your apostle panel, up to 10 VP.
3. Score 2 VP per upgrade in your hammer track and production tracks, up to 10 VP.
4. Score 3 VP per month dial with one of your workers on top, up to 10 VP.
5. Score 3 VP per zodiac dial with one of your workers on top, up to 10 VP.
6. Score 3 VP per workshop card in your play area, up to 10 VP.
7. Recover two workers (section 5.4.1).

Each hammer card grants a unique ability applied as the final step of construction. No hammer has an ability at Level I. Your hammer's level is determined by your cube's position on your hammer card.
Five hammer types (Level II and III effects):
Hammer A:
- Level II: Move your rooster disc up one position on your personal board. Take one gold and one point if your disc was already on the last position.
- Level III: Move your rooster disc up one position and gain 2 VP. Take one gold and one point if already on last position.
Hammer B:
- Level II: Correct one deviation.
- Level III: Activate the moon with a −1 modifier to what is indicated by your yellow mastery disc.
Hammer C:
- Level II: Move one mastery disc of your choice up one space.
- Level III: Move one mastery disc of your choice up one space and take one coin.
Hammer D:
- Level II: Take one coin from the general supply.
- Level III: Take one coin from the general supply and gain 2 VP.
Hammer E:
- Level II: Take one apostle in the usual way.
- Level III: Take one apostle and correct one deviation.

Scroll (red seal) effects — each scroll provides one of the following bonuses when flipped:
1. Take one apostle (section 8.3).
2. Activate the moon following the standard rules (section 8.4).
3. Move the painter following the standard rules (section 8.5).
4. Perform an upgrade (section 8.6).
5. Perform a construction (section 8.8).
6. Take one coin and move your rooster disc up one position on your personal board. Take one gold and one point if your disc was already on the last position.

chunks.section: Appendix — Rewards for Placing an Assistant
chunks.content:
When you place an assistant on a workshop card, the bonus shown in the centre of the card is one of the following:
1. Move your rooster disc up one position on your personal board. Take one gold and one point if your rooster disc was already on the last position of the track.
2. Take one apostle (section 8.3).
3. Activate the moon following the usual rules (section 8.4).

Josef Mánes can score up to 15 VP per scoring tile. Each tile shows a condition and a VP value per time it is met.
Five scoring tile types:
1. Score 3 VP for each month dial constructed with one of his workers on top, up to 15 VP.
2. Score 3 VP for each zodiac dial constructed with one of his workers on top, up to 15 VP.
3. Score 3 VP for each apostle in his play area at the end of the game, up to 15 VP.
4. Score 3 VP for each scroll or royal scroll in his play area at the end of the game, up to 15 VP.
5. Score 3 VP for each workshop card in his play area at the end of the game, up to 15 VP.

Rooster-related icons:
- Move rooster icon: Move the golden rooster one space to the right on the general rooster track.
- Trigger rooster's call icon: Immediately trigger the rooster's call at the end of the current player's turn (section 9).
- Rooster disc icon: Move your personal rooster disc up one position on your personal board. If your rooster disc is already on the last position, take one gold and one point instead of moving.

Resource icons:
- One basic resource icon (wildcard): Take one paint, wood, or iron of your choice.
- Specific resource icon: Take the indicated resource.
- Crossed-out resource icon: Pay (return) the indicated resource.
- Production track icon: Produce resources from a production track of your choice.
- Corresponding production track icon: Produce from the production track matching the action.
Deviation icons:
- Force deviation icon: Flip one deviation token to show the broken gear (section 5.3).
- Correct deviation icon: Flip one deviation token to hide the broken gear (section 5.3).
Worker action icons:
- Recover 1 worker: Recover one worker from the clock or calendar.
- Recover 2 workers: Recover two workers from the clock or calendar.
- Recover N workers: Recover the indicated number from the clock or calendar.
Additional action icons (can appear on cards or board sections):
- Coin + upgrade: Take one coin and perform an upgrade.
- Activate moon or move painter: Perform either action following standard rules.
